elements.text: Hint: Observe that if k ≥ 1 and f(x) is integer-valued for all sufficiently large x, then Af(x) is also integer-valued for all sufficiently large x. Represent f(x) in the form (11.1) and use induction on k.

elements.text: Let f(x) be a polynomial of degree k with complex coefficients. Prove that if f(x) is an integer for all sufficiently large integers x, then f(x) is an integer for all integers x.

elements.text: Let f(x) be an integer-valued polynomial and let m € Z. We define the polynomial fm (x) = f(x + m). Prove that f(x) and fm(x) are polynomials of the same degree and with the same leading coefficient. Let A(f) = {f (i) }i=. Prove that gcd (A(f)) = gcd(A(fm)).
